recommendation: A purpose-built, high-contrast coastal-safety screen that reads cleanly in one glance and is well-suited to a phone held in bright sun: warm off-white background, near-black ink, signage-style type, and a single safety-orange accent. It avoids every generic-AI design tell. It is scoped as the populated 'all good' view, so the data-loading state, the failed/stale tide-feed state, and the no-saved-walks empty state aren't designed yet — fine to ship this look now, but design those before it becomes a live app so a failed fetch can't quietly show out-of-date safety info.
assessed: design-system.md + .preview/current/index.html, with the rendered snapshot.png read directly; single-screen mobile success-state first look
